Braviary

#0627 Rufflet #0629 Vullaby
  • Contents
  • Info
  • Base stats
  • Evolution chart
  • Pokédex entries
  • Moves learned
  • Sprites
  • Locations
  • Language

Braviary is a Normal/Flying type Pokémon introduced in Generation 5.

Braviary has a Hisuian Form, introduced in Pokémon Legends: Arceus.

Braviary Hisuian Braviary

Additional artwork

Pokédex data

National № 0628
Type Normal Flying
Species Valiant Pokémon
Height 1.5 m (4′11″)
Weight 41.0 kg (90.4 lbs)
Abilities 1. Keen Eye
2. Sheer Force
Defiant (hidden ability)
Local № 0134 (Black/White)
0110 (Black 2/White 2)
0076 (Sun/Moon — Alola dex)
0090 (U.Sun/U.Moon — Alola dex)
0282 (Sword/Shield)
0222 (Legends: Arceus)
0366 (Scarlet/Violet)
0180 (The Isle of Armor)
0033 (The Indigo Disk)

Training

EV yield 2 Attack
Catch rate 60 (7.8% with PokéBall, full HP)
Base Friendship 50 (normal)
Base Exp. 179
Growth Rate Slow

Breeding

Egg Groups Flying
Gender 100% male, 0% female
Egg cycles 20 (4,884–5,140 steps)

Evolution chart

#0627
Rufflet
Normal · Flying
(Level 54)
#0628
Braviary
Normal · Flying
(Level 54, in Legends: Arceus)
#0628
Braviary
Hisuian Braviary
Psychic · Flying

Braviary changes

  • In Generation 8, Hisuian Braviary has Defiant as its hidden ability.
  • In Generations 5-7, Braviary has a base Friendship value of 70.

Pokédex entries

Braviary

Black They fight for their friends without any thought about danger to themselves. One can carry a car while flying.
White The more scars they have, the more respect these brave soldiers of the sky get from their peers.
Black 2
White 2		 For the sake of its friends, this brave warrior of the sky will not stop battling, even if injured.
X
Omega Ruby		 For the sake of its friends, this brave warrior of the sky will not stop battling, even if injured.
Y
Alpha Sapphire		 They fight for their friends without any thought about danger to themselves. One can carry a car while flying.
Sun With its brave disposition, it fears nothing—not even death. Ancient Alolan people respected it, referring to it as “the hero of the sky.”
Moon It’s thought that people disturbed their habitats in the past, so Braviary banded together to fight back.
Ultra Sun The more scars it has on its front, the more heroic it’s considered to be. Those with many scars on their back are mocked by the flock.
Ultra Moon Known as “the hero of the skies,” this Pokémon is so proud and so brave that it will never retreat, even when it’s injured.
Sword Known for its bravery and pride, this majestic Pokémon is often seen as a motif for various kinds of emblems.
Shield Because this Pokémon is hotheaded and belligerent, it’s Corviknight that’s taken the role of transportation in Galar.
Scarlet The more scars they have, the more respect these brave soldiers of the sky get from their peers.
Violet For the sake of its friends, this brave warrior of the sky will not stop battling, even if injured.

Hisuian Braviary

Legends: Arceus Screaming a bloodcurdling battle cry, this huge and ferocious bird Pokémon goes out on the hunt. It blasts lakes with shock waves, then scoops up any prey that float to the water’s surface.

Moves learned by Braviary

  • In other generations
  • 5

  • 7

Legends: Z-A Scarlet/Violet

Moves learnt by level up

Braviary does not learn any level up moves in Pokémon Legends: Z-A.

Moves learnt by TM

Braviary cannot be taught any TM moves in Pokémon Legends: Z-A.

Answers to Braviary questions

  • What is a good moveset for Braviary?
  • What is a good moveset for Hisuian Braviary?
  • How to get a braviary in pokemon black?
  • If I have Black, how can I see a Rufflet or Braviary for my pokedex?
  • Does Superpower's recoil affect Braviary's hidden ability of Defiant?
  • Why doesn't the ability Sheer Force cover the move Brave Bird?
  • Is Excadrill or Braviary better for BW2 playthroughs?
  • Why did Braviary show up at a Battle Tree battle?

Name origin

brave
courageous
aviary
a place where birds are kept
